title: Operation Epic Fury begins
description: US and Israeli forces launch coordinated strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities, IRGC command centers, and air defense networks. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed in strike on Leadership House compound in Tehran.

title: Iran retaliates on Gulf states and Israel
description: IRGC Aerospace Force launches ballistic missile salvos at US bases in Kuwait, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain. Simultaneous strikes on Israeli military targets including Nevatim Air Base and Ben Gurion Airport perimeter.

title: 6 US service members killed at Ali Al Salem, Kuwait
description: Fattah hypersonic missiles penetrate Patriot air defense coverage at Ali Al Salem Air Base. Six US service members killed, ten or more wounded. First US combat deaths of the conflict.

title: Oil markets surge
description: Brent crude spikes 18% in early trading as markets absorb the reality of a major Middle East conflict. Trading halted briefly on circuit breakers. WTI follows with 16% jump.

title: President Trump addresses the nation
description: Trump announces Operation Epic Fury from the Oval Office. Calls it 'a decisive action to prevent a nuclear Iran.' Pledges 'no ground troops' and 'total air dominance.'

title: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed
description: Iran deploys naval mines and activates anti-ship missile batteries along the Strait of Hormuz. IRGC Navy fast attack craft patrol the waterway. Commercial shipping diverts. Lloyd's of London suspends Gulf transit insurance.
data_point: 20% of global oil supply route closed

title: UN Security Council emergency session
description: Emergency UNSC meeting called. Russia and China condemn US strikes. US and UK defend action under self-defense and non-proliferation mandates. No resolution passed.

title: Hezbollah enters the war
description: Hezbollah launches massive rocket and missile barrage on northern Israel. Radwan Force units attempt cross-border incursion at Metula. IDF responds with airstrikes across Lebanon.
data_point: 500+ rockets launched at Israel in first 12 hours

title: Gas prices begin climbing across US
description: National average gas price rises to $3.25/gallon, up from $3.05 pre-war. Analysts project $4+ within weeks if Hormuz remains closed.

title: Houthi attacks on Saudi oil infrastructure
description: Yemen's Houthi forces launch drones and cruise missiles at Ras Tanura oil loading terminal in Saudi Arabia. Partial damage to loading platforms. Saudi Aramco reduces exports.

title: Minab girls' school struck — 23 children killed
description: Strike on IRGC ammunition depot near Minab causes secondary explosion that destroys adjacent girls' school. 23 children killed, 40+ wounded. Pentagon states school was not the target. Iranian state media broadcasts footage worldwide.

title: Global protests erupt
description: Mass anti-war protests in London, Paris, Berlin, Istanbul, Baghdad, and dozens of US cities. Estimated 2 million people march globally.

title: S&P 500 enters correction territory
description: US stock markets fall sharply as conflict escalation fears mount. S&P 500 down 8.3% from pre-war levels. Defense stocks surge; airlines collapse.

title: 14,000+ flights cancelled across Middle East and Europe
description: Airspace closures over Iran, Iraq, and parts of the Persian Gulf force massive flight cancellations. European carriers reroute around conflict zone, adding hours and fuel costs.

title: 450,000 displaced in Lebanon
description: UNHCR reports 450,000 people displaced in Lebanon as Israeli strikes intensify against Hezbollah positions. Refugee flows toward Syria and northern Lebanon overwhelm capacity.

title: US strikes Iranian oil infrastructure
description: In major escalation, US strikes Kharg Island oil terminal (90% of Iran's exports), Abadan refinery, and oil storage facilities. Stated objective: degrade Iran's ability to fund ongoing retaliation.
data_point: 90% of Iran's oil export capacity targeted

title: Brent crude breaks $100/barrel
description: Oil strikes push Brent past the $100 psychological barrier for the first time since 2022. WTI at $97. Analysts warn of $120+ if conflict extends beyond 30 days.

title: Strategic Petroleum Reserve release authorized
description: President Trump authorizes release of 30 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve over 60 days. Critics note this replaces less than 3 days of Hormuz flow.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei named Supreme Leader
description: Iran's Assembly of Experts names Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the killed Supreme Leader, as the new Supreme Leader. Mojtaba vows 'resistance until victory' and rejects any ceasefire.

title: Day 11 — War continues with no ceasefire in sight
description: Strikes continue across Iran. Hezbollah front intensifies. Oil at $101.50. Gas national average at $3.48 and climbing. Estimated US operational cost: $500M/day.

title: Oil crashes after Energy Secretary's false Hormuz claim
description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright posts on social media that the US Navy has escorted a tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. The claim is false — no escort occurred. Brent crude plunges 11.3% to $87.80 before partially recovering. The incident raises questions about information discipline within the administration during wartime.

title: Three ships hit in Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf
description: UK Navy reports three vessels struck by suspected projectiles: Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayruree Naree in the Strait off Oman, a container vessel west of Ras Al-Khaimah, and a bulk carrier northwest of Dubai. Attacks come after a 72-hour lull in maritime targeting.

title: Iran fires fresh missile barrage at Israel
description: Rocket trails seen over Netanya as Iran launches overnight ballistic missile salvo at Israeli territory. No direct impacts or injuries reported. Arrow and Iron Dome systems intercept all incoming. Fourth major Iranian salvo since Day 1.

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah command centers in Beirut suburbs
description: Israeli airstrikes target Hezbollah command centers and weapons sites in Dahieh (southern Beirut suburbs). Four killed in strike on central Beirut apartment linked to Hezbollah operations. Lebanese displaced now exceeds 700,000.

title: NATO shoots down Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey
description: Turkey confirms NATO air defenses shot down a ballistic missile in Turkish airspace fired from Iran — the first such incident in NATO history. The missile was likely targeting Israeli assets or a wayward retaliatory launch. Turkey had previously deployed a Patriot system to protect a NATO radar station in eastern Turkey.
data_point: First NATO intercept of Iranian missile

title: Trump sends mixed signals on war's end
description: President Trump tells CBS News the war is 'pretty much complete' but later tells House Republicans 'we haven't won enough.' Demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran. Mixed messaging roils markets and confuses diplomatic channels.

title: US death toll rises to 8
description: Pentagon confirms an eighth US service member has died from injuries sustained during an Iranian missile attack on US troops stationed in Saudi Arabia on March 1. The soldier had been in critical condition since Day 2.

title: Gas prices hit $3.63/gallon despite oil retreat
description: National average gas price rises to $3.63/gallon even as crude prices retreat. AAA notes gas prices lag oil by 7-10 days. California hits $4.81/gal. Analysts project continued climb as refinery capacity remains constrained by regional disruption.
